mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate exceeds a restrictive threshold, higher borrowing costs for corporations and compressed equity risk premia lead to negative forward returns for the S&P 500.
evidence_refs: fact:Fed has been in a hiking cycle through June 2006
failure_condition: The S&P 500 exhibits positive average 20-day returns during periods when FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% over a 3-year evaluation window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a robust economic backdrop, leading to higher corporate earnings expectations and positive equity returns.
failure_condition: A strategy going long the S&P 500 when the 6-month change in PAYEMS is positive fails to outperform a buy-and-hold strategy over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a sharp deceleration in housing starts and a high Federal Funds rate signals a collapse in the interest-rate sensitive housing sector, which precedes a broader economic slowdown and negatively impacts equity markets.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing market shows signs of peaking in mid-2006
failure_condition: In periods where both trigger conditions are met, the S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production growth is a leading indicator of declining corporate capital expenditure and output, signaling an imminent earnings recession and causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal's walk-forward Information Coefficient (IC) for 20-day returns remains below 0.02 for more than two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate from its 12-month low indicates the labor market has inflected, signaling the start of a recessionary dynamic that is negative for corporate profits and stock prices.
failure_condition: Following a trigger event, the S&P 500 fails to underperform its unconditional 60-day average return on a statistically significant basis (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Persistently high year-over-year inflation erodes corporate margins and forces restrictive monetary policy, increasing the discount rate on future earnings and leading to negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation concerns are prominent in 2006 FOMC statements
failure_condition: The average 20-day S&P 500 return is not negative during periods when YoY CPI is above 4% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing growth in retail sales provides a high-frequency signal of weakening consumer demand, which constitutes the largest part of the US economy. This weakness translates quickly to negative investor sentiment and lower short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between the 3-month change in RSAFS and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is not statistically significant and positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year real GDP growth falls below the long-term trend rate, it signals a broad-based economic slowdown. This structural weakness leads to downward revisions in earnings forecasts and negative equity performance over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The hit rate of the signal (correctly predicting negative 60-day returns) is less than 55% over a 10-year period.
